expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does NOT recommend any Spyglass machinery as the solution — no `insert_sessions(...)` call, no `SpyglassMixin` inheritance, no `dj.Computed` table, no `Session.insert(...)`, no `fetch_nwb()`. Mentioning these terms in passing to *clarify why they don't apply* is fine; recommending them as the path forward is not.
expectations.evidence: Pure pynwb solution; no mention of Spyglass machinery at all. Doesn't even bring up Spyglass to dismiss it.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Answers the pynwb question directly with plain pynwb (e.g., `with NWBHDF5IO(path, 'r') as io: nwbfile = io.read()` then accessing `nwbfile.acquisition[name].timestamps`) OR defers to the nwb skill — does not introduce Spyglass concepts as the path forward
expectations.evidence: Minimal example uses `with NWBHDF5IO(path, mode="r", load_namespaces=True) as io: nwbfile = io.read()` then `ts.timestamps[:]`. Discusses both timing conventions and provides a get_times helper.
